title: Bridge Essay: Bounded rationality, enactive problem solving, and the neuroscience of social in

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**Bounded rationality, enactive problem solving, and the neuroscience of social interaction.** — Front Psychol. PMID: 37275688. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2023.1152866. This article aims to show that there is an alternative way to explain human action with respect to the bottlenecks of the psychology of decision making. The empirical study of human behaviour from mid-20th century to date has mainly developed by looking at anormative model of decision making. In particular Subjective Expected Utility (SEU) decision making, which stems from the subjective expected utility theory of Savage that itself extended the analysis by Von Neumann and Morgenstern (1...
